mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS (e.g., Rank IC < 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If, after a trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year period, or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are declining, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy impacting interest-sensitive sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If this combined signal consistently fails to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, with an average positive return observed in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of high inflation, strong industrial production growth indicates an overheating economy, which forces the central bank to tighten, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during high inflation regimes, strong industrial production growth is followed by positive S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in GDP per capita signals a healthy and expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth (e.g., Rank IC < 0.03) over any 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls growth indicates a weakening job market, which foreshadows reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If, when PAYEMS growth falls below the threshold, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of stable or declining inflation, as measured by CPI, provides the Federal Reserve with room to maintain accommodative policy or ease, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods of low and stable inflation, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding and the unemployment rate is low, it signals efficient resource utilization and strong economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods of strong industrial growth and low unemployment, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
